skills.skill: Similar figures - apply the definition of similarity in terms of a dilation to identify similar figures and their proportional sides and the congruent corresponding angles;
skills.prompt: This topic covers similar figures (apply the definition of similarity in terms of a dilation to identify similar figures and their proportional sides and the congruent corresponding angles;) for Geometry students (TEKS G.7(A)). Congruent figures have the same shape and size; corresponding parts of congruent triangles are congruent (CPCTC). Similar figures have the same shape with proportional sides and congruent corresponding angles; AA criterion proves triangle similarity. Key vocabulary includes: AA criterion, CPCTC, congruent, corresponding parts, dilation, proportional, rigid motion, scale factor, similar. Students should practice with varied problem types, show clear mathematical reasoning, and verify their answers. Connect this topic to real-world applications and prior knowledge from Geometry. Common errors to watch for include computational mistakes, misapplication of definitions or formulas, sign errors, and incomplete reasoning or justification.
